subject: Peter, I wanted to introduce myself…
body:
Peter, My name is Sean Rose and I’m the Grassroots Engagement & Communications Advisor for Dr. Barresi’s campaign.

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/janet2020/i-wanted-to-introduce-myself?e=795c6d9796)

My name is Sean Rose, I am the Grassroots Engagement and Communications Advisor for Dr. Barresi’s campaign. First, and foremost, thank you for signing up to get involved with our campaign to return a conservative voice to Oklahoma’s fifth district! We are working hard to ensure we are engaged with our grassroots supporters so we can defeat Kendra Horn but we cannot do that without your support. I am here to be your point of contact to help get Janet elected on June 30th and thankful you have chosen to join Dr. Barresi’s winning team.

Dr. Barresi is the only candidate with 40 years of business experience, proven toughness against the radical left and is ready on day one to take on Nancy Pelosi, Kendra Horn and her socialist friends. We must send an experienced, tough and ready representative to Congress to ensure our conservative Oklahoma values are made known. We are absolutely thrilled and excited to have your support and work together to secure a win on June 30th.

I’ll be reaching out regularly to share specific opportunities and ways you will be able to help Janet secure a win for conservatism on June 30th. I encourage you to share this message with any friends & family that would be interested in helping Janet become our next congresswoman. A little goes a long way but we need as much involvement as possible. Please feel free to forward this email to them with my contact email Sean@Janet2020.com (mailto:Sean@janet2020.com?subject=Response%20to%20Volunteer%20Email) , or send them to our website so they can get signed up- janet2020.com/volunteer/ (http://www.janet2020.com/volunteer/?utm_source=Volunteers&utm_campaign=bfe1bdfb6e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2020_04_08_07_11&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_43d8936dc2-bfe1bdfb6e-395942890) .

Thank you for your support for Dr. Barresi and I will be in touch soon with more campaign trail updates!

Respectfully,

Sean Rose
Grassroots Engagement & Communications Advisor
Janet Barresi for Congress 2020

subject: Will you join my campaign to root out corruption in Washington?
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Josh Hicks for Congress
body:
Did you see this message from my team, friend? I hope you’ll take a minute to read it over.

This campaign, from the very beginning, has always been about serving regular folks — Kentuckians left behind by bought-and-paid-for politicians like Andy Barr and Mitch McConnell.

We’re building a grassroots movement that will, for the first time in a long time, give the families of Kentucky’s 6th District a real, honest voice in Washington.

But I can’t do it alone — if you’re able, will you sign up for one of our new volunteer teams? Take a look at the email I’m forwarding to see some ideas for how you can help out.
https://secure.ngpvan.com/r5iYnMTLcEWy_tZ9nNQI2A2

No matter how you get involved, I’m so grateful for your support. It’s people like you that drive this campaign forward.

-- Josh

---------- Forwarded message ----------

From: Team Hicks
Date: April 10, 2020
Re: After yesterday's planning meeting, we're ramping up our volunteering options

Friends —

Over the last few weeks, we’ve talked a lot about how our campaign is adapting — our team is practicing social distancing and we’ve canceled all in-person events.

Normally, we’re out driving from corner to corner of our district so Josh can hear from as many people as possible.

With a “Healthy at Home” order, our team is getting creative, and we’re ready to let you know how you can get involved in growing #TeamHicks!
We’re looking for folks who are interested in helping out — whether that’s sewing face masks for essential workers, volunteering with local non-profits, or helping spread the word about Josh and our campaign through social media.

No matter what role you’re able to take on, our volunteer teams will help us get more folks engaged and harness the power of our grassroots supporters (that’s you!).

Next , we need you to sign up to join one or several of our volunteer teams.

Whether you’re an expert seamstress or a savvy social media user, we need your help. And if you have other ideas, we want to hear those, too.

SIGN UP TO VOLUNTEER! [https://secure.ngpvan.com/r5iYnMTLcEWy_tZ9nNQI2A2]

Thank you for being a part of this team — no matter how you can get involved. Whether that’s reading this email, becoming a donor, or joining one of our #TeamHicks volunteer teams, we’re proud to have you on board.

-- Team Hicks

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-apr?refcode=HIC_20200411_em_volsurvey2_org_all_ftr1

Josh Hicks for Congress

Josh Hicks is a Democrat running to represent Kentucky’s 6th Congressional District. He will be a representative who puts the needs of his constituents front and center in Washington. Josh grew up in Fleming County before leaving to serve in the Marines. After two deployments, he returned to Kentucky to serve his community as a police officer and attorney.
He’s running to represent hardworking, everyday Kentuckians. Not corporations. That means his campaign is relying on grassroots donations — lots of small donations from emails like this — not big checks from corporate special interests. name: Congressman Al Green
subject: [Sign on and forward] No water shutoffs during a pandemic
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Keep Al Green in Congress
body:
| [](https://act.myngp.com/el/6rrt34hrDUwsMKhmLSSnb-4FQe1yCNMZC31j08p8Oh8=/zKrAvh6xlvd-4ZlYcprPG921jweuEouNvZv25YN6ogU=)
---
| Folks, A couple of weeks ago, I asked you to share with me how COVID-19 is impacting you and your family. Some of the most poignant stories I heard were from folks who were worried about having essential utilities like water shut off because they couldn't afford to pay their bills. **Right now, over 2 million Americans don 't have access to running water in their homes.** At a time when frequent handwashing is the CDC's number one recommendation to prevent the spread of COVID-19, that is simply unacceptable. [**Will you join me in calling on state and national leaders to prevent and reverse all water shutoffs for the duration of this public health crisis, and then forward this email to a friend?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/6rrt34hrDUwsMKhmLSSnb-4FQe1yCNMZC31j08p8Oh8=/9bURj76Wtc-kCB16KXc2JMnN0Yp1HO6LyyDE_vxQqCA=?source=group-keep-al-green-in-progress&referrer=group-keep-al-green-in-progress&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Falgreen.org%2F) | **[SIGN NOW& FORWARD TO A FRIEND](https://act.myngp.com/el/6rrt34hrDUwsMKhmLSSnb-4FQe1yCNMZC31j08p8Oh8=/9bURj76Wtc-kCB16KXc2JMnN0Yp1HO6LyyDE_vxQqCA=?source=group-keep-al-green-in-progress&referrer=group-keep-al-green-in-progress&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Falgreen.org%2F)**
---
Several states have already committed to a moratorium on water shutoffs -- but it's not enough. We need nationwide legislation guaranteeing that no American will be without clean, safe water during this crisis, and that all homes currently without water will have services restored.
Folks, this pandemic has exposed many of the faults in our systems. Too many American families lack healthcare, savings, and even access to essential services like water. And now, our country's failures in caring for the most vulnerable among us is a public health concern that affects us all.
[**It 's time to take action. Will you sign your name next to mine and join me in calling on our elected leaders to prevent and reverse water shutoffs throughout the COVID-19 pandemic?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/6rrt34hrDUwsMKhmLSSnb-4FQe1yCNMZC31j08p8Oh8=/9bURj76Wtc-kCB16KXc2JMnN0Yp1HO6LyyDE_vxQqCA=?source=group-keep-al-green-in-progress&referrer=group-keep-al-green-in-progress&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Falgreen.org%2F)
**[SIGN NOW& FORWARD TO A FRIEND](https://act.myngp.com/el/6rrt34hrDUwsMKhmLSSnb-4FQe1yCNMZC31j08p8Oh8=/9bURj76Wtc-kCB16KXc2JMnN0Yp1HO6LyyDE_vxQqCA=?source=group-keep-al-green-in-progress&referrer=group-keep-al-green-in-progress&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Falgreen.org%2F)**
---
Thanks for speaking up,
Congressman Al Green

Right now, over 2 million Americans don 't have access to running water in their homes. At a time when frequent handwashing is the CDC's number one recommendation to prevent the spread of COVID-19, that is simply unacceptable. | Several states have already committed to a moratorium on water shutoffs -- but it's not enough. We need nationwide legislation guaranteeing that no American will be without clean, safe water during this crisis, and that all homes currently without water will have services restored. Folks, this pandemic has exposed many of the faults in our systems. Too many American families lack healthcare, savings, and even access to essential services like water. And now, our country's failures in caring for the most vulnerable among us is a public health concern that affects us all. Thanks for speaking up, Congressman Al Green | | Congressman Al Green has served the people of Texas' 9th district for 15 years. He is a progressive leader in Washington and was the first Member of Congress to introduce articles of impeachment against President Trump on the House floor.

I wanted to take the time to quickly wish you and your loved ones a very Happy Easter! Peter, I wanted to take the time to quickly wish you and your loved ones a very Happy Easter! This Holy occasion reminds us that the sufferings of the present age are temporary, but Christ’s love is eternal. Obviously Easter is a bit different this year due to the nationwide precautions in place to minimize the spread of coronavirus, but you can still celebrate; it will just be a little different. Many churches are hosting live-streamed worship services on television or the Internet. And you can still make Easter baskets, dye eggs, and celebrate with those you love. We are all facing uncertainty during these challenging times. I strongly encourage you to maintain a positive outlook toward new beginnings and find solace in God's love this Easter weekend. **_Acts 4:33 — With great power, the apostles continued to testify to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus. And God's grace was so powerfully at work in them all._** God bless, David Hill AUTHORIZED AND PAID FOR BY DAVID HILL FOR OKLAHOMA, INC | | body:
In response to personal messages from doctors and nurses pleading for assistance... | | | Dear Friend,

Earlier this week, we called on Gov. Murphy to immediately allow doctors the option to prescribe Hydroxychloroquine (HCQ) as a preventative medicine when COVID-19 is suspected. This medication has improved the condition of COVID-19 patients within hours and has been credited as life saving.

On behalf of the numerous doctors, nurses and frontline healthcare professionals I’ve spoken to, I again urge Gov. Murphy to put politics aside and support our medical teams’ efforts to save lives.

Please see our press release below for details. As always, I will continue to fight for our country, our state, and for you.

Sincerely,
Hirsh Singh

# Hirsh Singh Calls for Gov. Murphy to Allow Medical Community Preventative Use of Hydroxychloroquine to Treat COVID-19 Patients

**LINWOOD, NJ** — In response to many conversations with doctors and nurses pleading for assistance, Republican candidate for U.S. Senate Hirsh Singh has issued a statement calling for Governor Phil Murphy to start allowing healthcare professionals to use preventative medicines (prophylactically) as an option to protect themselves and their patients when COVID-19 infection is suspected if they choose.

“The current policy not only drastically increases the risk to our nurses, doctors, and other first responders and will degrade our medical capability but will also place individuals waiting for test results in a dangerous and possibly deadly window where we could save their lives. News of medical professionals becoming sick and, in some cases, dying is extremely demoralizing to everyone and really harms medical care while limiting our ability to ensure we immediately take corrective action for a patient that may be infected just doesn’t make sense,” said Singh.

“No one knows the situation better than our frontline medical professionals. I’ve got doctors and nurses telling me ‘Hirsh, if we know something works and has minimal side effects, why are we not allowed to keep our staff and our patients safe?’ So I will back them up all the way. I sincerely hope that Governor Murphy will immediately reconsider his decisions in this regard.”

Researchers have recently found that the anti-malarial drug Plaquenil (generic: hydroxychloroquine) suppresses symptoms of COVID-19. Even though New Jersey is struggling to contain over 44,000 cases of COVID-19 and has suffered more than 1,200 deaths, Governor Murphy has accepted AG Grewal’s statewide restriction on Plaquenil and other medicines. Currently, patients are only allowed controlled dosages in the most extreme cases, and these restrictions do not permit the use of Plaquenil and other drugs as preventative measures. Tests can take days to return results and we have seen many cases where people have died while waiting.

Further emphasizing the lifesaving benefits of hydroxychloroquine, State Representative Karen Whitsett (D-MI) praised President Trump on Monday for raising awareness of the drug after she’d tested positive for coronavirus in March. According to Whitsett, she experienced relief “less than two hours” after taking the medication. New Jersey State Senator Joe Pennacchio has also called for prophylactic use of hydroxychloroquine.

“I call on Governor Phil Murphy to support our frontline medical professionals in containing COVID-19 with minimum losses at once by removing their restrictions on hydroxychloroquine,” says Singh.

###

Every day, we’re finding new and creative ways to stay connected with supporters like you and keep folks engaged with our campaign because there’s still an important election underway. But with everything that’s going on right now, it comes as no surprise that our fundraising numbers have taken a significant dip. clean_body: | Hello — Just as this public health crisis has undoubtedly changed your life, it has changed our campaign, too. My team and I are working from home after moving all of our outreach and fundraising online. It was the right decision, and I’m proud of my team for adapting to this new reality so quickly. Every day, we’re finding new and creative ways to stay connected with supporters like you and keep folks engaged with our campaign because there’s still an important election underway. But with everything that’s going on right now, it comes as no surprise that our fundraising numbers have taken a significant dip. And just as it happened, we got word that Mitch McConnell’s super PAC booked $67 million in ads targeting Democrats in key Senate races. That makes grassroots dollars from folks like you more important to flipping the Senate than ever before. In this new reality, it will take all of us working together to win this election. So, I’m asking: If you’re financially able, can you add a donation to help us muster the resources we need to build a different kind of campaign — one that can take on Mitch McConnell and flip the Senate? Anything you can give will go a long way. name: Team Warner
subject: an update on COVID-19
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
|
---
| | | If you would like more information about how to slow and stop the spread of COVID-19, **[take a look at these resources from the Centers for Disease Control (CDC).](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d50d/53d9897e/54da0fa4/1c899f82/143382435/VEsH/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-ncov/index.html")**
---
Derek --

Team Warner hopes that you and your families are staying safe and healthy during this outbreak. We are committed to bringing you updates on what Mark Warner is working on to make sure that all Virginians are protected during this difficult time.

Here are just some of Mark's priorities from this week:
* **[Mark sponsored the Natural Disaster and Emergency Ballot Act,](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d50d/53d9897e/54da0fa4/1c899f81/143382435/VEsE/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://www.nbc29.com/2020/04/08/senator-mark-warner-sponsors-natural-disaster-emergency-ballot-act/")** which would expand vote-by-mail and early voting access across the country. In Virginia, we've already made great strides to expand voting access, and Mark wants to make sure that everyone has the same opportunity to cast their ballot as we look forward to November.

* As daily and weekly newspapers across the Commonwealth announce cuts and unpaid leave, Mark stood with Tim Kaine and other Senate colleagues to ask for **[funding to support local journalism and media outlets](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d50d/53d9897e/54da0fa4/1c899f80/143382435/VEsF/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://www.wavy.com/news/business/sens-warner-and-kaine-urge-funding-to-support-local-journalism-amid-pandemic/")** in the next coronavirus relief package. This outbreak has made the already essential role of local news even more important.

* Mark joined a bipartisan push to make sure our **[farmers and rural communities are protected](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d50d/53d9897e/54da0fa4/1c899f8f/143382435/VEsC/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://www.marketscreener.com/news/Jeff-Merkley-Merkley-Wyden-Colleagues-Urge-Administration-to-Ensure-Continuity-of-Food-Supply-by--30346655/")** during the coronavirus pandemic by suspending pending foreclosures and issuing loan payment deferrals, among other short-term relief provisions.

* Mark led a bipartisan group of senators in calling for **[immediate assistance for mortgage servicers](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d50d/53d9897e/54da0fa4/1c899f8e/143382435/VEsD/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://www.wavy.com/news/national/senators-call-for-assistance-to-mortgage-servicers-to-avoid-housing-finance-system-crisis/")** to avoid a housing finance system crisis and make sure that the mortgage relief provided in the CARES Act can be actualized for homeowners.

A lot of folks across the Commonwealth celebrated Passover this week or are celebrating Easter this weekend, and we'd like to extend our warmest wishes for happy and safe holidays. It's a difficult time to be apart from family and loved ones, and we hope that folks are able to connect virtually now, and are looking forward to a time to be together in person once it's safe to do so. We will get through this together.

Be well,
Team Warner
|

subject: Whatever you celebrate, have a happy holiday
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY MARGARET GOOD FOR CONGRESS
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17114172/182858090/-940294256?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83ODIwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzMjBlMDliNC0yNjdjLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eVcoTqniRzI4hlQbEC1eiMyxZaR5qyqnyOYQpqtgyyM=&emci=6ecb6e4d-057c-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=320e09b4-267c-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=9814644) Hi there, Spring is here, which symbolizes rebirth, renewal, and the return of summer weather! It also means that people across the globe are celebrating holidays like Passover, Easter, and at the end of this month, Ramadan. Celebrations may look different this year: Passover Seders were celebrated on Zoom, and Holy Week and Easter services have moved online. But especially now, during this time of so much uncertainty, holidays that bring us together -- even if not physically -- are exactly what we all need. For our neighbors here in Southwest Florida, these holidays are a celebration of new beginnings, hope, and generosity. For those who celebrate, I hope these last few days and next few weeks are a time for peace and happiness, and a reminder that our friends and loved ones are still here to share that with us, even if we can’t all be together physically. Happy holidays to everyone who celebrates, and I look forward to the time we can all be back in the community together. \-- Margaret

| | | [**Help for America's Small Businesses and Their Workers**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=957d75c7b5&e=3ee4985eea)
-Fox Business
“We want workers to have work, not to become dependent on the unemployment system,” Labor Secretary Eugene Scalia and Small Business Administrator Jovita Carranza write. President Trump’s new “Paycheck Protection Program addresses this by offering [100% forgivable] small business loans of up to $10 million.”

**🇺🇸****President Trump:** [America Works Together!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0a6fcdc88e&e=3ee4985eea)

-_New York Post_
This week, Senate Democrats blocked a simple but urgent request to boost emergency relief for U.S. small businesses from $350 billion to $600 billion. “Everything is an opportunity,” House Speaker Nancy Pelosi said, previewing Democrats’ plan to use the small business money as leverage for other items on their political wishlist.

I'm happy to share some good news with you today.
#
Almost a month ago, our movement to elect Charles Booker for Senate moved 100% online due to the COVID-19 pandemic. We were all unsure about what this new reality would look like. And as the campaign's Field Director, I was nervous about what this move could mean for our volunteers and their outreach.
That's why, today, I'm happy to share that our campaign has over 200 volunteers calling voters to talk about Charles and the race every week. I am incredibly thankful for our volunteers and their time and energy.
These folks are Kentuckians committed to a brighter future for all of us, and their work is vital to our campaign's success. It'll only become more vital as we get closer to the primary. body:
I wanted to send a quick message to wish you a very Happy Easter! Peter, I wanted to send a quick message to wish you a very Happy Easter! Although Easter will be a little different this year due to the global pandemic, we can still celebrate by remembering that Jesus rose from the dead. Without a doubt, we will get through these challenging and uncertain times together. Due to nationwide COVID-19 precautions, many churches have made their services available online, either through a live stream video or a recorded podcast. If your place of worship does not have streaming capabilities, feel free to “visit" another church instead this Easter Sunday. My wife and I would love to have you join us for the James River Church service. Just click the following link for more information: [https://jamesriver.church/.](http://email.hawleyformo.com/u/click?_t=11a592fdc01846d4a1c728421295853d&_m=b13156c205e247c88b2e72a83a4fc395&_e=CvmTELfeJeu4k9HUeTvVWrtdMEWCT63nGjK_bQA0PxPzBBP7iL_6Mt63MjgkckGSTHDI3BeKTPREK-QGrBIWFLHvDRKkPRCkBZrQ3smUQlpiSoXOqHpCEa09vCdeo4s8AQqpJBDKaWqHEgFldQebbHqLKepbtMdjdDQdMtmTlnXVXRauncPFZXDCh3Q4kJSZJkkLfXRkL3p7h8WnUIlS9XfKLskHAMqc_cAYSuLVVYZty-5rgCyApJLO33sK9qyUlsFkvMG6eSFbxjl3OiqKT3XTRsM3c39iFfpuEyGSJLhbxKBljR2k63o5HrksiBdZXpDZAfA333JwNgoJUwtwJiGchutSI2nHT5t0TJk81Lg%3D) I encourage you to look forward to new beginnings this Easter. body:

Peter,

Thank you to everyone who has already joined us for our Virtual Town Halls: It has been meaningful to virtually gather as a community during this time. I hope you'll join us for another discussion this Tuesday evening with Eda Henries about how small businesses are adapting to this challenge and her initiative providing financial consulting services to small businesses affected by COVID-19. I also hope you'll forward this opportunity to small businesses in your community and submit questions you need answered on our registration form.

with Eda Henries
Volunteer Coordinator, [covid19bizrelief.com](https://act.myngp.com/el/q7L-nLseIqC3wMvJNNVyqRQ4HnHvqH7FZSge-MrI7uo=/dQz5rUND2wadmB4aiPFQfei1UvZQmGyymEaRsax5Xos=)
Founder and President, Henries & Co.
Chief Financial Officer, Cornbread Farm to Soul

Tuesday, April 14, 2020
6:30-7:30PM EST
Zoom conferencing information will be shared with registered participants.

******[CLICK HERE TO RSVP](https://act.myngp.com/el/q7L-nLseIqC3wMvJNNVyqRQ4HnHvqH7FZSge-MrI7uo=/UR22lTt42xtcIQnBnRyfWTlBtKmh2NeQCcY0pPklj4Q=)******

Eda is an inspiring member of our community who utilized her extensive global finance and venture capital expertise to launch [covid19bizrelief.com](https://act.myngp.com/el/q7L-nLseIqC3wMvJNNVyqRQ4HnHvqH7FZSge-MrI7uo=/i2UGIgzjTRrF64YG6YYbmecMWjFzRt2QilSmRejd__8=), a nationwide volunteer group of individuals who got together to help U.S.-based businesses who need it during the COVID-19 pandemic. These volunteers come from varying professional backgrounds, including entrepreneurs, consultants, bankers, financial, legal, and business industries. They're here to help mentor and consult with business owners to better understand the various federal, state, city, and private relief programs. You can follow their efforts on [Twitter @covid19relief](https://act.myngp.com/el/q7L-nLseIqC3wMvJNNVyqRQ4HnHvqH7FZSge-MrI7uo=/jgNuatYDhvR281vQ5i1ubl_UNuB7oTc59PnvIkkQRL0=) and [Instagram @covid19bizrelief](https://act.myngp.com/el/q7L-nLseIqC3wMvJNNVyqRQ4HnHvqH7FZSge-MrI7uo=/tmnOT4Vyl32JdpfnU3IZyOjvsJg1qEyVVA5q0N9S7tE=). Eda is also the Founder and President of Henries & Co. and the Chief Financial Officer of Cornbread Farm to Soul. I look forward to Eda sharing her knowledge with our small business community and answering some of your questions.

Every day, it is inspiring to see creativity being utilized to help those who are most vulnerable. Thank you for being a part of our community, and as always, please reach out if our team can be of any help.

Best,
Adem

| | Peter --

We emailed you yesterday to let you know about our urgent new fundraising goal.

* Sending mailers to folks in the district that tell the truth about our opponent's promises to be a cheerleader for Trump, no matter what.
* Recruiting volunteers to help us call voters across CA-25 to connect with them about this election.

This work is vital to us holding onto this seat -- but we can't do it if we don't raise the funds to pay for it.

**[We _really_ need to raise $100,000 by TONIGHT to put this plan in motion. subject: you've been specifically chosen, friends
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warnock for Georgia
body:
| | |
---
We just pulled a list of our top supporters - the people most dedicated to flipping Georgia blue and taking back the Senate - and your name is on that list, friends.
Our Republican opponent - unelected Sen. Kelly Loeffler - has just been accused of insider trading. You've been _specifically_ chosen because you have valuable insights that we believe can help defeat her in November. **[_So please, friends, will you help us develop a winning strategy by taking our short campaign strategy survey now?_](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://surveys.signforgood.com/warnock-april-campaign-survey?firstName=&lastName=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=30004&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk129-su-nd")**
| | [Take the Survey](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://surveys.signforgood.com/warnock-april-campaign-survey?firstName=&lastName=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=30004&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk129-su-nd")
---
Nate Silver's FiveThirtyEight reports that Democrats CAN take back the Senate - but only if we win in Georgia. That's why it's so important that we hear directly from supporters like you.
While Kelly Loeffler (R) is already spending millions of her own fortune to try and buy this seat, Reverend Warnock (D) is running a grassroots campaign that puts your interests, your priorities and your concerns front and center.
